antipattern.symptom: Mock is set up; test calls the service; mock.assert_called_once() FAILS with "Expected to be called once. Called 0 times." The real Stripe API is silently hit.
antipattern.root-cause: Patch target is `stripe.Charge.create` (definition site), but the service did `import stripe` and has its own local name `apps.payments.services.stripe`. The patch never affects the service's local binding.
antipattern.fix: Patch at the consumer's import path: `@patch("apps.payments.services.stripe.Charge.create")`. Detector: validator rejects patch_target not starting with "apps.".

antipattern.symptom: Test asserts that User was created. The mock returned the expected object. But no row exists in the DB — the service's create() was never tested.
antipattern.root-cause: Test patched `User.objects.create` to return a Mock. The DB layer was bypassed entirely.
antipattern.fix: Use the real ORM with the db fixture + factories. Mocks belong at external boundaries, not at the ORM. Spec anti_mocks MUST include "django-orm".

antipattern.symptom: Rate-limit test passes (sleep is mocked) but the rate-limiter never resets in production. Bug found post-deploy.
antipattern.root-cause: `time.sleep` was patched to a no-op. The retry / rate-limit state machine relies on real time passing.
antipattern.fix: Mock the rate-limiter or retry decorator directly, NEVER `time.sleep`. Spec anti_mocks MUST include "time-sleep".

antipattern.symptom: First few hundred tests pass; tests deeper in the run fail because the cached EXPIRY is now in the past relative to the test's frozen clock.
antipattern.root-cause: Module-level `EXPIRY = datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)` is evaluated once at import; subsequent tests see the same stale value.
antipattern.fix: Move time-relative calculations inside the test function. Use freezegun if a fixed clock is needed. Detector: grep `^[A-Z_]+ = datetime\.now\(` in test modules.

antipattern.symptom: Test "passes" but the assertion is meaningless — the code path that should have called the mock was actually skipped (early return) and the mock's return_value was used by some unrelated default.
antipattern.root-cause: Test asserted only on the mock's return value or downstream effects, never on whether the mock was actually invoked.
antipattern.fix: Always use `mock.assert_called_once_with(...)` or `mock.assert_called()`. Spec rule rejects asserts that lack a call-verification keyword.
